column8: George orwell wrote books than any other 20th century writer.
column9: As a student at Eton, he soon showed how clever he was.
column10: As a young man, Orwell left England and became a policeman.
column11: Orwell wanted to do more than journalism in his early writing career.
column12: People living in poor conditions often praised Orwell for his work.
column13: His time in Spain changed his feelings about working-class people.
column14: Orwell wrote Nineteen Eighty-Four years after writing Animal Farm.
column15: Orwell wanted his books to be used in classrooms.
